key_insight: InvDesMobility adds a reliability gate to inverse materials design so only independently validated, evidence-backed first-principles results are fed back into the closed loop.
engineering_relevance: This makes expensive DFT-driven discovery more auditable and robust for properties like carrier mobility, where intermediate quality signals matter as much as the final scalar prediction.
method: Multi-agent automated DFT produces and verifies candidate structures and mobility channels.
Evidence is stratified into retained feedback objects: relaxed structures update the generative model, while reliable mobility channels train acquisition ranking.
A feedback contract controls admission, provenance, and validation priority across iterations.
evidence_signal: On 516 2DMatPedia-derived candidates, the workflow yielded 280 QC-passed materials, 573 retained carrier-direction seed channels, screened 2.4×10^6 structures, and submitted 102 candidates for DFT validation.
watch_item: The approach depends on careful implementation of reliability thresholds and audit workflows, which may reduce throughput or bias feedback if the gating criteria are too strict.
paper_title: InvDesMobility: a reliability-gated first-principles feedback framework for closed-loop materials discovery
